reviewRun.reviewPolicy: Review the human-align-surfaces queue for whether each claim truly needs human alignment, deterministic proof, human confirmation, or decomposition. Keep evidenceStatus unknown unless direct evidence is present.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The claim is a broad product-ownership boundary over packet/status semantics. It is important for a human to confirm against CLI docs, packet schemas, and current implementation before treating it as proven.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Human-confirm the packet/status ownership boundary against CLI reference, packet schemas, and implementation before promoting narrower deterministic checks.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is an implementation behavior about active-run workspace allocation and sticky shell state. It should be proven by deterministic runtime tests rather than human alignment alone.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect a deterministic active-run test that proves the workspace root is pinned per workflow and reused through the shell environment variable.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is a cross-surface product boundary claim. It requires human or maintainer alignment across binary, bundled skill, adapter contract, and consumer adoption docs before proof would be honest.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Human-review the binary/skill/adapter boundary and split any executable subclaims into deterministic or dev/skill proof.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is an ownership boundary over provider-error interpretation. It is not a fixture target until the public packet and consumer adapter behavior are aligned.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Confirm the provider-error ownership boundary against live-run packets and adapter docs; split concrete schema checks if needed.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The workspace directory and prepare-hook timing are runtime behavior and should be covered by deterministic invocation tests.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect deterministic live-run invocation tests for workspace directory allocation and one-time prepare hook timing.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is an ownership boundary over workspace contents. It needs alignment across product runtime and consumer-owned workspace behavior before proof would be honest.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Human-confirm which workspace contents are product-owned versus consumer-owned, then split any observable no-write guarantees into deterministic tests.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is a packet-shape and adapter-template behavior that can be checked deterministically.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect deterministic schema/template checks proving workspace lifecycle is not exposed through the public packet shape and flows through adapter placeholders.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is a maintained external-consumer proof claim. The review input does not include direct evidence refs for the external commands, so it should become human confirmation or evidence-linking work rather than generic alignment.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Human-confirm or attach direct evidence for the external consumer evaluator and pytest runs referenced by the maintainer note.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is an external adoption proof claim. It needs direct evidence attachment or human confirmation, not broad alignment.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Human-confirm the external adoption proof or attach the matching consumer proof artifact before treating it as satisfied.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is a repository structure claim about where implementation surfaces live. It can be checked deterministically by inspecting command ownership and source layout.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect a deterministic source-layout or command-registry check for Node-owned bootstrap/release/provider wrappers.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is a product/consumer ownership boundary and should remain human-auditable alignment work until docs, adapters, and examples agree.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Human-review the Cautilus-versus-consumer ownership boundary and split executable subclaims into deterministic or eval proof.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is a binary-versus-skill ownership boundary. It still needs alignment against the bundled skill, contract docs, and actual command surfaces before proof would be honest.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Human-confirm the binary/skill ownership boundary, then split executable skill orchestration behavior into dev/skill scenarios.
